win: fix mkdir returning EPERM instead of EEXIST on network drives
prs.body:
### Summary

This PR fixes an issue where `uv_fs_mkdir()` returns `UV_EPERM` instead of
`UV_EEXIST` on Windows network-mounted filesystems (SSHFS, SMB, FTP, etc.)
when attempting to create a directory that already exists.

On NTFS, `CreateDirectoryW()` returns `ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS (183)`, but on
network drives it often returns `ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED (5)` even when the
directory already exists. libuv currently maps `ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED` to
`UV_EPERM`, causing incorrect behavior.

This patch adds a check using `GetFileAttributesW()`: if the path exists and
is a directory, libuv now returns `UV_EEXIST`, matching expected semantics.

**This incorrect error code affects:**

- `fs.mkdir()` on Windows network drives  
- `fs.mkdir({ recursive: true })`  
- `fs-extra.ensureDir()`  
- any code relying on `EEXIST` to detect an existing directory  

Returning `EPERM` breaks application logic in many real scenarios.


### Fix

When `CreateDirectoryW()` fails with `ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED`, libuv now checks:

```c
DWORD attr = GetFileAttributesW(req->file.pathw);
if (attr != INVALID_FILE_ATTRIBUTES &&
    (attr & FILE_ATTRIBUTE_DIRECTORY)) {
  req->result = UV_EEXIST;
  return;
}
```

If the directory truly exists, return UV_EEXIST.
Otherwise, keep the original UV_EPERM behavior.

**Result:**
Network drive existing dir → returns UV_EEXIST (correct)
NTFS existing dir → unchanged
Real permission errors → unchanged
No changes to non-Windows platforms

**Related Issue:**
Fixes: #4959

feat: add console tool with runtime and builder log panel
prs.body:
**Description:**
- This PR adds a developer console panel to the playground interface, as requested in issue #5. It enables users to view, debug, and manage both runtime logs and builder diagnostics while editing code.

**Features Implemented:**

1. ConsolePanel component

  - Captures `console.log`, `console.warn`, `console.error`, and `console.info`
  - Displays logs in a styled, scrollable panel
  - Includes a tab-based UI for:
    - Console logs
    - Builder logs

2. Builder log system

  - Allows programmatic logging of compile-time info like auto-save or setting updates
  - Accessible via `pushBuilderLog()` method

3. UI Features

  - Tabs for log type switching
  - Clear logs button
  - Auto-updating console feed
  - Integrated beneath the Preview panel

prs.title: chore(warn): include key name in inject() usage warning
prs.body:
- This PR improves the developer warning message in inject() when used outside setup() or functional components.

**Before:**

 inject() can only be used inside setup() or functional components.

**After:**

 inject("userStore") can only be used inside setup() or functional components.

- This makes it easier for developers to identify which injection key caused the warning.

- solves issue #13287

prs.title: Added Find Maximum problem with implementations and formatted all files
prs.body:
**Added:**
- Solutions for Find Maximum in Array in cpp, csharp, golang, javascript, typescript, and python
- README.md with problem statement, examples & test cases

 **Details:**
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(1)

**Formatting:**
- Python → black
- JS/TS → prettier
- Go → go fmt
- C++/C# → clang-format

Solves issue #5

### **User description** - Added CircularProgressIndicator on home screen during video upload. - Implemented loading states on workout history and progress charts. - Added shimmer effects and loading messages for smoother transitions. - Ensured consistent loading design across all screens. - Solved issue #72 ___ ### **PR Type** Enhancement ___ ### **Description** - Added comprehensive loading components library with shimmer effects - Implemented loading indicators across all screens (home, progress, workouts, AI chat) - Added skeleton loaders and full-screen loading states for async operations - Improved error handling with retry actions and descriptive messages - Created detailed documentation guide for loading components usage ___ ### Diagram Walkthrough ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Loading Components
loading_overlay.dart"] -->|"LoadingWidget"| B["Home Screen"] A -->|"ShimmerStatCard"| C["Progress Screen"] A -->|"ShimmerWorkoutCard"| D["Workouts Screen"] A -->|"VideoUploadLoading"| E["AI Chat Screen"] A -->|"FullScreenLoading"| C A -->|"LoadingListSkeleton"| D F["shimmer package"] -->|"provides effects"| A B -->|"displays"| G["Loading States"] C -->|"displays"| G D -->|"displays"| G E -->|"displays"| G ```

Project now uses Tailwind CSS for a modern, utility-first design.

* Added explicit types for props, state, and handlers.

prs.title: Redesigned buttons with modern styling and hover effects
prs.body:
**Enhanced Button Design with Glassmorphism Effect:**

- Solved Issue #25 
- Glassmorphism Design: Implemented transparent gradient buttons with backdrop blur for a modern frosted glass effect
- Consistent Styling: Applied uniform purple gradient (#667eea to #764ba2) across all language buttons with matching shadows and borders
- Interactive Animations: Added smooth hover effects (lift, scale, shimmer), active states, and cubic-bezier transitions for premium feel
- Responsive Layout: Optimized grid system for desktop, tablet (≤768px), and mobile (≤480px) with 2-column layout on small screens
- Accessibility: Added focus indicators, rel="noopener" security, prefers-reduced-motion support, and high contrast mode compatibility
- Typography Update: Changed heading to pure white for better contrast and removed unnecessary bold formatting from footer

prs.title: Set up tech-news route
prs.body:
**What i did:**

- Solved issue #134

- Created a separate Node.js backend in the /Backend folder to securely fetch tech news using NewsAPI.

- Exposed an endpoint /api/news to serve news data to the frontend without exposing the API key.

- Configured .env and vercel.json to make the backend Vercel-ready.

- Modified Homecontent.jsx to fetch news from /api/news?q=coding (instead of directly using NewsAPI).

- News section now loads safely and works correctly on both local and production environments.

- Ensured Firebase config now uses environment variables with fallbacks.

- Added .gitignore, .firebaserc, and firebase.json for proper Firebase setup isolation.

prs.title: Made the forget password option functioning
prs.body:
**What I Did:**

- Implemented working "Forgot Password" logic using Firebase Authentication.

- Integrated sendPasswordResetEmail from Firebase to actually send reset emails.

- Updated the handleForgotPassword function to:

       1. Validate email input

       2. Display success and error messages based on Firebase response

       3. Auto-close the reset form after successful reset request

- Ensured proper UI feedback using message box for success/error

- Cleaned up duplicate imports and ensured error handling covers invalid/missing emails

**Testing:**

- Successfully tested with valid and invalid emails

- Checked proper message display on error and success

- Verified that reset email is sent and received
